steps.title: Recognize the exponential growth form
steps.content: When a population grows by a fixed percentage each year, use the model $p(t) = p_0 \times r^t$, where $p_0$ is the initial population and $r$ is the growth factor per year. The initial population at $t = 0$ (year 1788) is 24 jackrabbits.

steps.title: Convert the annual percentage to a growth factor
steps.content: A 16.2% increase per year means multiply by $1 + 0.162 = 1.162$ each year. The growth factor is $r = 1.162$.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content:
Substitute $p_0 = 24$ and $r = 1.162$ into the general form:

$$p(t) = 24 \times 1.162^t$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
